channel.title: mozilla on T++
channel.description: Recent content in mozilla on T++

item.title: Mozilla's new CEO
item.description: I am a fan of Popehat, a legal blog that focuses primarily on free speech issues. As they like to put it social actions, the many varieties of “speech” in the US, are protected by the 1st amendment. Unlike many parts of the Constitution these days we take the 1st amendment pretty seriously. The government is very limited in what it can do to you for your speech. “Protected” speech, in this sense, means protected from government initiated consequences (like jail or fines).
